color.$description:
Arena — Dravensoft skin (palette), dark theme.
This is the source you swap to re-skin Arena. It holds only skin values:
the daisyUI --color-* set per theme and the categorical chart ramp.
Everything else — the Arena aliases, the muted-text derivations — lives in
tokens/colors.css and follows automatically.
After changing anything here, rebuild and re-run: bun scripts/check-ramp.mjs

error.$description:
Outline danger: this red IS the text and the border, so it is tuned against
the base surfaces, lighter than the shared #e53e3e it replaced (4.29:1 on
base-200 — under AA).

error-fill.$description:
Filled danger. --color-error is tuned to be read AS TEXT on the base
surfaces, which makes it far too light to carry white text itself. The one
filled danger surface (ConfirmDialog's final confirmation) therefore takes
its own fill, dark enough for --color-error-content. The two cannot be one
token: the outline needs a lighter red and the fill a darker one, in
opposite directions. This pin is OPTIONAL — omit it and colors.css derives
the fill from --color-error by darkening in oklab; the Dravensoft skin pins
it for an exact tone. Both gates are machine-checked — check-text-contrast.mjs.

cat-1.$description:
Categorical chart ramp — identity only, never meaning (see README → Theming).
Fixed order: slot N is always slot N. A 9th series folds to "Other", small
multiples, or direct labels — it is NEVER a generated hue.
Chroma is capped at OKLCH C <= 0.15 so the ramp sits in Arena's register
(crimson .177, gold .100) instead of reading as neon.
forest  136°
